sections.heading: Issue
sections.body: Review the supplied documents and produce a first-pass internal memo covering parties, obligations, deadlines, signatures, and any data-quality flags a human operator/reviewer should verify before relying on the draft.

sections.heading: Document Set Overview
sections.body: - The supplied evidence includes multiple unrelated document types: a blank AO 88B subpoena form, a magistrate-judge consent/reference form excerpt, a Form D filing instruction excerpt, a notice of claim of lien, and extracted fields for a renotice to take video deposition. [5aaabcf14b21fd40:p1:c0] [a72d9aa7f0bed8eb:p1:c1] [18aae1ac9b6c1191:p1:c2] [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0] [d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields]

citation_quotes.5aaabcf14b21fd40:p1:c0: AO 88B (Rev. 02/14) Subpoena to Produce Documents, Information, or Objects or to Permit Inspection of Premises in a Civil Action
citation_quotes.a72d9aa7f0bed8eb:p1:c1: Consent to a magistrate judge’s authority.
citation_quotes.18aae1ac9b6c1191:p1:c2: Where to File: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
citation_quotes.d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields: document_type: Renotice to Take Video Deposition

sections.heading: Subpoena Form
sections.body:
- The AO 88B subpoena page is an uncompleted federal civil subpoena form directed to production of documents, information, objects, or inspection of premises. [5aaabcf14b21fd40:p1:c0]
- The subpoena form contains blank fields for the district, civil action number, recipient, production place, and production date/time. [5aaabcf14b21fd40:p1:c0]

citation_quotes.5aaabcf14b21fd40:p1:c0: SUBPOENA TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS, INFORMATION, OR OBJECTS OR TO PERMIT INSPECTION OF PREMISES IN A CIVIL ACTION

sections.heading: Subpoena Obligations
sections.body:
- The subpoena form states that the recipient is commanded to produce materials at the time, date, and place set forth in the form, but the supplied excerpt does not show completed compliance details. [5aaabcf14b21fd40:p1:c0]
- The subpoena form also includes an inspection-of-premises command option requiring entry at a stated time, date, and location, but those fields appear blank in the supplied text. [5aaabcf14b21fd40:p1:c0]

citation_quotes.5aaabcf14b21fd40:p1:c0: YOU ARE COMMANDED to produce at the time, date, and place set forth below

sections.heading: Subpoena Service
sections.body: - The subpoena proof-of-service page is uncompleted and includes blanks for receipt, service, returned-unexecuted reason, fees, date, server signature, printed name/title, and server address. [5aaabcf14b21fd40:p2:c0]

sections.heading: Subpoena Deadlines
sections.body: - The attached Rule 45 excerpt states that objections must be served before the earlier of the compliance time or 14 days after service. [5aaabcf14b21fd40:p3:c2]

citation_quotes.5aaabcf14b21fd40:p3:c2: The objection must be served before the earlier of the time specified for compliance or 14 days after the subpoena is served.

sections.heading: Magistrate Consent Form
sections.body:
- The magistrate-judge consent form excerpt states that consenting parties would allow a United States magistrate judge to conduct all proceedings, including trial, final judgment, and post-trial proceedings. [a72d9aa7f0bed8eb:p1:c1]
- The magistrate consent form includes blank areas for printed names of parties and attorneys, signatures, and dates. [a72d9aa7f0bed8eb:p1:c1]
- The reference-order language states that the case would be referred to a United States magistrate judge under 28 U.S.C. § 636(c) and Fed. R. Civ. P. 73, but the supplied excerpt does not show a completed judge signature or date. [a72d9aa7f0bed8eb:p1:c1]

citation_quotes.a72d9aa7f0bed8eb:p1:c1: The following parties consent to have a United States magistrate judge conduct all proceedings in this case including trial, the entry of final judgment, and all post-trial proceedings.

sections.heading: Form D Filing Instructions
sections.body:
- The Form D excerpt states that five copies of the notice must be filed with the SEC, with one manually signed copy. [18aae1ac9b6c1191:p1:c2]
- The Form D excerpt gives the SEC filing address as 450 Fifth Street, N.W., Washington, D.C. 20549. [18aae1ac9b6c1191:p1:c2]
- The Form D excerpt states that there is no federal filing fee. [18aae1ac9b6c1191:p1:c2]

citation_quotes.18aae1ac9b6c1191:p1:c2: Copies Required: Five (5) copies of this notice must be filed with the SEC, one of which must be manually signed.

sections.heading: Lien Notice Parties
sections.body: - The lien notice identifies HARBOR VIEW HOLDINGS, LLC as the filing party and NORTHSTAR MARKET LLC as the party against whom the notice is filed. [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0]

citation_quotes.e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0: Filed By: HARBOR VIEW HOLDINGS, LLC Against: NORTHSTAR MARKET LLC

sections.heading: Lien Notice Property
sections.body: - The lien notice describes the property as Lot 14, Block C, Willow Industrial Park, Plat Cabinet 7, Slide 22. [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0]

citation_quotes.e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0: Property: Lot 14, Block C, Willow Industrial Park Plat Cabinet 7, Slide 22

sections.heading: Lien Notice Recording
sections.body: - The lien notice lists Cause No. 2024-CV-00318 and recording information as Book 4922, Page 071. [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0]

sections.heading: Lien Notice Amounts
sections.body: - The lien notice states that the lien is for unpaid access road maintenance charges for November 2023 and December 2023, in the aggregate principal amount of $2,850.00. [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0]

citation_quotes.e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0: unpaid access road maintenance charges covering the months of NOVEMBER 2023 and DECEMBER 2023

sections.heading: Lien Notice Demand And Cure
sections.body:
- The lien notice states that demand for payment was served by certified mail on January 9, 2024, but the tracking number is partially unclear in the source text. [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0]
- The lien notice states that the fifteen-day cure period prescribed by the demand has expired. [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0]

citation_quotes.e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0: Demand for payment was served by certified mail on January 9, 2024

sections.heading: Lien Notice Signatures
sections.body: - The lien notice signature block names HARBOR VIEW HOLDINGS, LLC and M. Alvarez, Authorized Member, but the signature line and notary date appear blank in the supplied text. [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c1]

citation_quotes.e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c1: HARBOR VIEW HOLDINGS, LLC By: ______________________________ M. ALVAREZ, Authorized Member

sections.heading: Lien Notice Data Quality
sections.body: - The lien notice includes handwritten review annotations indicating that the amount, certified mail number, and a possible filing date should be verified. [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c0] [e7a7d9482447de83:p1:c1]

sections.heading: Deposition Renotice Parties
sections.body: - The derived-fields evidence identifies the document type as a renotice to take video deposition involving multiple New York Supreme Court, New York County matters. [d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields]

citation_quotes.d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields: document_type: Renotice to Take Video Deposition

sections.heading: Deposition Renotice Deadlines
sections.body: - The derived-fields evidence states that the deposition was scheduled to begin January 23, 1998 at 10:00 a.m. and continue until complete. [d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields]

citation_quotes.d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields: Deposition to begin January 23, 1998 at 10:00 a.m. and continue thereafter until complete.

sections.heading: Deposition Renotice Obligations
sections.body: - The derived-fields evidence states that the person to be examined was required to produce specified documents at least five days before the examination. [d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields]

citation_quotes.d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields: The person to be examined is required to produce specified documents at least five (5) days before the examination.

sections.heading: Deposition Renotice Dates
sections.body: - The derived-fields evidence states that the deposition notice was dated December 15, 1997 at New York, New York, with fax/header artifacts appearing to show 12/16/97. [d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields]

citation_quotes.d61bba9a2aabfec3:fields: document dated December 15, 1997 at New York, New York

sections.heading: Open Questions
sections.body:
- Are the AO 88B subpoena and AO 85 magistrate-consent excerpts merely blank standard forms, or were completed pages omitted from the supplied evidence?
- For the lien notice, what is the complete certified mail tracking number after “9489 0090 0027 6148 11__?”
- For the lien notice, what is the actual dated execution day in February 2024 and was the document signed and notarized?
- For the lien notice, should the handwritten note “file by 03/15?” be treated as an internal reviewer note, an actual deadline, or something else?
- For the lien notice, what is the referenced “2019 esmt,” and does it affect the claimed maintenance charges or property description?
- For the deposition renotice, the available evidence is extracted fields rather than source-page text; the original notice should be checked before relying on names, index numbers, production obligations, or dates.
- For the Form D excerpt, what issuer/offering information, if any, appears on omitted pages?
- Do the supplied documents belong to a single matter or were they retrieved as unrelated examples from different files?

evidence.text: AO 88B (Rev. 02/14) Subpoena to Produce Documents, Information, or Objects or to Permit Inspection of Premises in a Civil Action UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT for the __________ District of __________ ) ) ) ) ) ) Plaintiff v. Civil Action No. Defendant SUBPOENA TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS, INFORMATION, OR OBJECTS OR TO PERMIT INSPECTION OF PREMISES IN A CIVIL ACTION To: (Name of person to whom this subpoena is directed) ’ Production: YOU ARE COMMANDED to produce at the time, date, and place set forth below the following documents, electronically stored information, or objects, and to permit inspection, copying, testing, or sampling of the material: Place: Date and Time: ’ Inspection of Premises: YOU ARE COMMANDED to permit entry onto the designated premises, land, or other property possessed or controlled by you at the time, date, and location set forth below, so that the requesting party may inspect, measure, survey, photograph, test, or sample the property or any designated object or operation on it. Place: Date and Time: The following provisions of Fed. R. Civ. P. 45 are attached –

AO 88B (Rev. 02/14) Subpoena to Produce Documents, Information, or Objects or to Permit Inspection of Premises in a Civil Action (Page 2) Civil Action No. PROOF OF SERVICE (This section should not be filed with the court unless required by Fed. R. Civ. P. 45.) I received this subpoena for (name of individual and title, if any) on (date) . ’ I served the subpoena by delivering a copy to the named person as follows: on (date) ; or ’ I returned the subpoena unexecuted because: . Unless the subpoena was issued on behalf of the United States, or one of its officers or agents, I have also tendered to the witness the fees for one day’s attendance, and the mileage allowed by law, in the amount of $ . My fees are $ for travel and $ for services, for a total of $ . I declare under penalty of perjury that this information is true. Date: Server’s signature Printed name and title Server’s address Additional information regarding attempted service, etc.:

not appear in person at the place of production or inspection unless also commanded to appear for a deposition, hearing, or trial. (B) Objections. A person commanded to produce documents or tangible things or to permit inspection may serve on the party or attorney designated in the subpoena a written objection to inspecting, copying, testing, or sampling any or all of the materials or to inspecting the premises—or to producing electronically stored information in the form or forms requested. The objection must be served before the earlier of the time specified for compliance or 14 days after the subpoena is served. If an objection is made, the following rules apply: (i) At any time, on notice to the commanded person, the serving party may move the court for the district where compliance is required for an order compelling production or inspection. (ii) These acts may be required only as directed in the order, and the order must protect a person who is neither a party nor a party’s officer from significant expense resulting from compliance. (3) Quashing or Modifying a

without adverse substantive consequences. The name of any party withholding consent will not be revealed to any judge who may otherwise be involved with your case. Consent to a magistrate judge’s authority. The following parties consent to have a United States magistrate judge conduct all proceedings in this case including trial, the entry of final judgment, and all post-trial proceedings. Printed names of parties and attorneys Signatures of parties or attorneys Dates Reference Order IT IS ORDERED: This case is referred to a United States magistrate judge to conduct all proceedings and order the entry of a final judgment in accordance with 28 U.S.C. § 636(c) and Fed. R. Civ. P. 73. Date: District Judge’s signature Printed name and title Note: Return this form to the clerk of court only if you are consenting to the exercise of jurisdiction by a United States magistrate judge. Do not return this form to a judge.

of the date it is received by the SEC at the address given below or, if received at that address after the date on which it is due, on the date it was mailed by United States registered or certified mail to that address. Where to File: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, 450 Fifth Street, N.W., Washington, D.C. 20549 Copies Required: Five (5) copies of this notice must be filed with the SEC, one of which must be manually signed. Any copies not manually signed must be photocopies of the manually signed copy or bear typed or printed signatures. Information Required: A new filing must contain all information requested. Amendments need only report the name of the issuer and offering, any changes thereto, the information requested in Part C, and any material changes from the information previously supplied in Parts A and B. Part E and the Appendix need not be filed with the SEC. Filing Fee: There is no federal filing fee. State: This notice shall be used to indicate reliance on the Uniform Limited Offering Exemption (ULOE) for

NOTICE OF CLAIM OF LIEN Pursuant to State Mechanics' and Materialmen's Lien Act Cause No. 2024-CV-00318 Recorded: Book 4922 Page 071 Filed By: HARBOR VIEW HOLDINGS, LLC Against: NORTHSTAR MARKET LLC Property: Lot 14, Block C, Willow Industrial Park Plat Cabinet 7, Slide 22 [handwritten: CHECK w/ R. Singh] TO ALL PERSONS having or claiming any interest in the above-described real property, NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the undersigned, HARBOR VIEW HOLDINGS, LLC, a limited liability company organized under the laws of this State, claims a lien upon the property described above. The lien is asserted for unpaid access road maintenance charges covering the months of NOVEMBER 2023 and DECEMBER 2023, together with incidental charges, in the aggregate principal amount of $2,850.00 (Two Thousand Eight Hundred Fifty and 00/100 Dollars). Demand for payment was served by certified mail on January 9, 2024, certified mail tracking number 9489 0090 0027 6148 11__?, and remains unsatisfied as of the date of this notice. The fifteen (15) day cure period prescribed by the foregoing demand has expired. [handwritten: $2,850 -- confirm] [handwritten: verify

9, 2024, certified mail tracking number 9489 0090 0027 6148 11__?, and remains unsatisfied as of the date of this notice. The fifteen (15) day cure period prescribed by the foregoing demand has expired. [handwritten: $2,850 -- confirm] [handwritten: verify cert mail #!] Claimant reserves the right to commence a judicial action to foreclose this lien and to recover all sums due, together with costs, interest, and reasonable attorneys' fees, in accordance with applicable law.
